title: neogaillonia incana
intro: Neogaillonia incana, also known as the 'Wild Madder', is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Asia. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and infections. The plant's health benefits include reducing fever, relieving pain, and improving digestion, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. Neogaillonia incana can be used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
